steps.text: Prep: Finely dice onion, bell pepper, and celery. Mince garlic. If using frozen crawfish tails, thaw and drain well; reserve any liquid for the sauce if desired.

Make a blond roux: In a heavy skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat, melt butter. Sprinkle in flour and whisk until smooth. Cook, whisking frequently, until the roux turns blond/golden and smells nutty (do not let it get dark like gumbo roux).
Saute the trinity: Stir in onion, bell pepper, and celery. Cook until softened and the mixture looks glossy. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Season: Stir in Cajun seasoning, paprika, thyme, black pepper, and salt. Cook 30 seconds to bloom spices.
Build the sauce: Add stock gradually while stirring to keep the sauce smooth. Add Worcestershire and optional hot sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer to thicken: Reduce heat to low and simmer until the sauce thickens to a spoon-coating consistency. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Add crawfish gently: Stir in crawfish tails and simmer just until heated through (avoid hard boiling so the crawfish stays tender).
Finish bright: Stir in lemon juice, green onions, and parsley. Taste and adjust salt, Cajun seasoning, and hot sauce.
Serve: Spoon etouffee over hot cooked rice. Garnish with reserved green onions and parsley.
